additional_unit_test.name: protocol::tests::test_parse_notification_has_no_id
additional_unit_test.purpose: Lock down that JsonRpcRequest deserializes notification frames (no id) with id == None

verified_pre_fix_line_numbers.protocol.rs:31: pub id: Value (non-optional, no #[serde(default)]) — matches triage
verified_pre_fix_line_numbers.server.rs:34-41: process_request always returned String written to stdout regardless of incoming id — matches triage

fix_summary.protocol.rs: Changed pub id: Value to pub id: Option<Value> with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] (also added doc comment explaining notification semantics).
fix_summary.server.rs: (a) Added route arm for "notifications/initialized" (replacing legacy bare "initialized" — never spec-correct per MCP 2024-11-05). (b) Refactored process_request signature String → Option<String>: returns None for notifications (id is None), Some(json) for requests. (c) Refactored run() to skip writeln! when process_request returns None. (d) Notification with wrong jsonrpc version is silently dropped (cannot respond by definition). (e) Notification dispatched through any handler (including unknown method) drops the result without emitting a response.
fix_summary.lib.rs: Made `mod server` `pub mod server` (was private with only `pub use server::run` exposed) so integration tests in tests/mcp_lifecycle_test.rs can call process_request directly. Updated module-level docstring to reflect the new public surface.

spec_decision.decision: REPLACED bare 'initialized' (NOT kept as legacy alias)
spec_decision.rationale: Per MCP 2024-11-05 lifecycle spec, the canonical client→server post-handshake notification method is 'notifications/initialized' (namespaced). The bare 'initialized' route was never spec-correct (no MCP draft used it) — it was a v0.1.x typo. Spec-compliant clients (Claude Code, mcp-python-sdk) send only the namespaced form. Keeping the legacy route would silently accept malformed clients while masking the underlying spec violation. Removed cleanly.

red_reason_gate.red_stdout_excerpt_notification_test: panicked at crates/tldr-mcp/tests/mcp_lifecycle_test.rs:57:5: notification frame produced response (must be None per JSON-RPC 2.0 §4.1): Some("{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"error\":{\"code\":-32700,\"message\":\"missing field `id` at line 1 column 54\"},\"id\":null}")
red_reason_gate.red_stdout_excerpt_lifecycle_test:
assertion `left == right` failed: expected exactly 2 emitted frames (initialize + tools/list, no response for notification), got 3: [<initialize ok>, <parse error 'missing field `id` at line 1 column 54' for notification>, <tools/list ok>]
  left: 3
 right: 2
red_reason_gate.notes: Gate (c) 'unknown method' is not exercised because the bug short-circuits earlier at deserialization (gate (a)). Gates (a) and (b) both fire on the lifecycle_handshake_three_frames panic — the unwanted middle frame contains the literal substring 'missing field `id`'.

spec_gotchas_for_m4.initializeresult_snake_case_observed_in_: During the lifecycle_handshake_three_frames RED panic the captured initialize response shows the literal string '"protocol_version":"2024-11-05"' and '"server_info":{"name":"tldr-mcp"...}' — confirming M4's bug pre-condition. M4 must change InitializeResult fields' serde rename, not the JsonRpcRequest struct M3 owns.
spec_gotchas_for_m4.notification_method_naming: MCP 2024-11-05 uses 'notifications/initialized'. There is NO legacy alias in the spec — bare 'initialized' was a v0.1.x typo, not a spec form. M4's broader audit should similarly reject any other bare-method-name (no slash) routes if it discovers them while walking the response surface.
